_comment: Balance study: on clay, Ace (idx 4) vs Ace (idx 4). Side A (you) plays Topspin only, side B (opp) plays Flat only, both Slice on the return of serve. The objective rewards parameter sets where A's per-MATCH win rate is near 60% (topspin the slightly-better percentage play), with a sample-size penalty so a near-60% seen over too few matches does not score well. First server alternates by seed so the 60% reflects shot merit, not serve order. Copy to e.g. autotune.topspin.config.json and run: .venv-autotune/bin/python scripts/autotune/tune.py --config autotune.topspin.config.json . Every number here is editable.
_fitness_note: 60% win rate stays the primary objective (weight 1.0, robust). The realism guards are STRENGTHENED (higher weights, tighter ranges) so the search cannot reach 60% via degenerate short/winner-heavy play: a solution that hits 60% but with e.g. avg_rally 1.6 / winner 71% now costs far more than a realistic 60%. Because the win-rate penalty grows quadratically with distance from 60, being far off is still punished most; the guards break ties among near-60% candidates toward realistic tennis. If no realistic ~60% region exists, the study will report the best compromise (that itself is useful signal).
